Code fragment: public final TableField<JGithubCredentialsRecord, String> BASE_URL = createField(DSL.name("base_url"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR(255).defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("'https://api.github.com'::character varying"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR)), this, "");
Explanation: The BASE_URL column has a default value but is marked as NULLABLE, which is inconsistent. If the column should always have a value (via the default), it should be NOT NULL; otherwise, the default value may mask the distinction between 'not provided' and 'explicitly set to default'. Consider adding .nullable(false) to the column definition.

Code fragment: public final TableField<JGithubCredentialsRecord, Boolean> IS_ACTIVE = createField(DSL.name("is_active"), SQLDataType.BOOLEAN.defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("true"), SQLDataType.BOOLEAN)), this, "");
Explanation: The IS_ACTIVE column has a default value of 'true' but is marked as NULLABLE, which creates ambiguity between NULL and false states. Since this is a boolean flag with a default, it should be NOT NULL to ensure consistent behavior. Add .nullable(false) to the column definition.
